lines.text: The heroic spirits of the moon and stars have long seen through it. These hollow lies taint Septimont's sacred arena.
Guilty.
Augusta, banish the Silvas from Septimont!
Guilty. The Temple has spoken.
Your performance just now... That wasn't a complete disaster. At least, you show more promise than those idiots retreating to cling to their meager points.
An outlander... Hmph. Fight well, both of you.
Do not disappoint me or Augusta in tomorrow's final.
Hmm? That's interesting.
Augusta, look! Another two have earned Arsinosa's blessing.
Aha! See, Augusta? Told you we'd make it in time!
Septimont's mess is mostly sorted now. The Senate can deal with the rest. We don't do rushed goodbyes around here. No matter how far, we had to come send you off.
And don't go forgetting us either, got it?! We're throwing a huge celebration in Septimont for this victory, and the Ragunnesi will be there too. So you'd better show up!
Our would-be Hero of Heroes, lost in daydreams again? What is it this time? Staring at an empty wall, hoping one day it'll bear your name?
Inconsequential? Come now. Everyone knows you're the Senate's golden child. Those old loons wouldn't spare me a glance, but they hang on your every word.
Have you been waiting long? Ugh, I can't believe he got to you first.
Hmph. I'm not blind to your tricks, Avidius. Do you really think {PlayerName} would take sides? With you?
Let me guess... Eliminating Tyrannos and the Dark Tide before anyone else would be the perfect act to prove you're the prophesied Hero of Heroes, hmm?
Four expeditions to <te href=750010>Manes Deep</te> plus a <te href=750011>Holmleaf Medal</te>, and now you think you can usurp Augusta's place? Don't make me laugh.
Oh, read the room, will you?
Why you...
Well, there you have it. That's the gist of it. Ugh, finally some time together, and now it's all ruined...
It all started when Augusta shook things up the moment she became Ephor. Especially in rooting out corruption within the Gladiator's Agon while ignoring the senators in the process.
Naturally, it ruffled some feathers. Some even accused her of "undermining the spirit of Gladiating." In reality, they're just upset their piggy banks got smashed.
More importantly...
This is exactly the kind of chaos Augusta wanted. That idiot!
She even went so far as to say, "The citizens of Septimont only need to be loyal to Septimont, not her, Augusta."
And then, like it's nothing, she tells me that the Ephor position only matters when a strong Gladiator wants it.
She's even more stubborn than I am.
Now why would I be worried about that? I'm sure you've seen her sword. The inlaid blades are trophies of every Gladiator she's defeated.
Even ones with decent Resonance powers like Avidius, she eats for breakfast.
Though... Avidius makes me rather uncomfortable. It's got nothing to do with his stance, it's... more of a hunch. Something about him feels... unnatural.
Or maybe I'm just overthinking it.
It's the <te href=750012>Griffrex</te>! It appears our prey has finally come out for some fresh air.
We'll meet again later. But next time, I'd like your undivided attention. No distractions, hmm?
Perfect timing.
I'll stretch my legs a bit.
I wonder what Grandmother Lillibet and {PlayerName} are talking about...
Never mind. It can wait.
Let's get eyes on this beast, Aquila.
Spirits of the hallowed land. Answer my call.
May the eternal Sacred Flames grant us boundless sight.
You can set me down here.
Come on... Don't give me that concerned look of yours. It's nothing to worry about.
Wait. You have questions. Don't pretend you don't.
Well, spit it out. It's only a minor injury, I can manage.
So you've managed to "glimpse" that far? Or is there more?
Heh. Just how many more surprises do you have for me? But I suppose only you could manage something like that.
Grandmother Lillibet, the Head Priestess you met at the Temple, once spoke of the "unknowable void" that is your fate.
I've yet to fully interpret what she meant by that, but one thing is clear. You are the most unique among us all.
They were... as I once perceived through the Sacred Flames... The fate of Septimont...
Just as you saw, the Dark Tide will bring destruction to Septimont, and... the death of Augusta.
I don't know whether Ragunna's predictions are actually true, but fate has given me no response but sorrow.
Though I would deny it to the end, as it is now... I cannot ignore it any longer. The Dark Tide is a force to be reckoned with.
Its terror lies not in the illusions it shows, or the lies it spins. Rather, it uncovers the parts of us we hide the most carefully. The parts of ourselves we least wish to face.
Never. My prophecies are never wrong.
How I... loathe this feeling. This helplessness. To know everything, to see everything, and yet be powerless to change a thing...
Since we've come this far, might as well share a secret with you.
The Iuno you see before you can no longer see the threads of fate.

I gave up my ability to peer into fate in "exchange" for the Resonance power to resist the Dark Tide. Why? Because I refused to be fate's obedient puppet, bowing to its whims and letting it dictate my every step.
But in the end... despite all the sacrifice, we still couldn't escape its grasp...
The dark figure we faced in Asphodel Barrens was, in fact, an embodiment of the Dark Tide. We call it the "False Sovereign."
Unlike other Dark Tide creations, it can devour and reshape the scattered powers of its maker.
I'm unclear how things will unfold, but I'm certain it's the source of evil that will bring about the fall of Septimont, just as the prophecy foretold.
Before this hunt, Augusta and I investigated such Dark Tide irregularities at the Sanguis Plateaus.
We then formed a plan to take advantage of the Sovereign's nature, using the Corrosaurus as bait to lure out its imperfect form...
Maybe this way, we can rewrite Septimont's tragedy before it even begins.
But as you can see, we failed...
I do not know...
Fate doesn't lie. Perhaps death is the only choice we're given in the end.
But knowing Augusta... I'm sure that fool would fight till the bitter end.
There is one thing... I'm not certain about. For reasons I can't explain, the fate shown to me by the Dark Tide feels different from what I remember.
What is it exactly? Ugh...
A side effect of my Resonance power.
The Dark Tide doesn't exactly show up in a stable form. My powers manifest its creations in exchange for a teensy piece of my "existence." That's the price to pay, I suppose.
Ugh. I admit it sounds a bit complicated. In short, just know that a little sleep will relieve these side effects.
A fair point. Once I've recovered my strength, sorting out these questions will be as simple as a flick of a finger.
Oh, by the way, take this.
A Lunarum bracelet containing a small portion of my Resonance power. It was passed down to me by my predecessor when I became a Priestess.
It's quite dear to me, you know, so be sure to take extra good care of it. And don't expect there will be a spare if you lose it.
Rely on it in my absence. Who knows, it may even help fill in for me and help you and Augusta in my place.
Now, why would you say that?
I'll only be taking a short nap! Don't you or Augusta even dare think about leaving me behind, is that clear?
I must admit, this place is rather magnificent.
I suppose my predecessors forsook the peace they ought to have enjoyed, just to share this hard-earned land with these hunters. Such magnificence is rightly owed to the <te href=750005>Temple</te>.
Tch...
Though we've already met, I suppose a proper introduction is in order.
I am Iuno. Priestess of the Tetragon Temple.
Well, aren't you caught up on things? What you say is true, though I've long since grown tired of such affairs.
Come now, {PlayerName}. Surely you can find me something more interesting to occupy my time. For instance... the hunt?
Quite frankly, when it comes to dealing with the Dark Tide, none of these Gladiators can hold a candle to me.
Ha. Look at those looks. I've seen them all before.
What can I say? When you're as dazzling as I am, jealousy tends to follow.
I'll be making my way back, Augusta. Lest my shine further sting the eyes of certain people.
If you find yourself with free time later, consider visiting me at Tetragon Temple.
Not every outlander is granted access to its sacred halls. Make the most of it.
A field of corpses. Horrifying, isn't it?
This is Tyrannos's birthplace. A kingdom of brutality, and the beast is proud of it.
Every hunt before this... it was here that we met our final battles.
The Gladiators went to great lengths to drive the Dark Tide's creatures into the Asphodel Barrens. Likewise, their prey were just as eager to bait their hunters here.
This is destined to be an evenly matched game of life and death. Hunter or prey? Only the one still standing gets to decide.
Finally, some fun for a change.
Come. You need only to stay at my side, {PlayerName}. Rest.
This is a matter between Augusta and Avidius. We shall not intervene for the time being.
Steady... Now!
Augusta...
Augusta...
If, and I say if... Septimont's fate has already been decided.
Would you continue to fight...?
Go chase your fate... Augusta...
Augusta...
Augusta.
Terrible timing... Is there any other way to reach her?
Tch, how vexing.
The Dark Tide's inside...
If we feed that monster something else…
Right, the Anchor Stone!
Very sharp. But before we act, there are two things we need to prepare.
First, we'll need to get the Sovereign to open up and swallow the Anchor Stone like a good boy. That will be on you and Augusta, {PlayerName}.
Second, the Dark Tide itself must be stabilized to keep the Sovereign nice and solid. My job, naturally. No one else is fit for the task.
I'm not made of glass, Augusta.
I am Iuno, the most gifted Priestess Septimont has seen in generations. If anyone can hold back the Dark Tide, it's me. And I'm not going to fall on you that easily.
Besides, the Sacred Flames already showed me my end. In the warmth of spring, surrounded by white flowers, with gentle eyes watching over me. But that day is still far from today.
At worst, I'll just need more time to recover. Everything's going to be alright. I promise.
Then it's settled. I'll let you prepare. Just give me a signal when you're near the Earthrender.
I won't be able to hold the Sovereign's form for long, so make the most of that window.
Did you forget anything, {PlayerName}?
Useful, isn't it? Keep it close. No matter where you go, a part of me goes with you, got it?
Since I've given you a tool so practical, I'll ask something in return. Just one thing.
Promise me you'll stay by Augusta's side, and face whatever destiny sets upon you. Together. This isn't a command or a suggestion. It's... a personal request.
Only you can do this.
I think I understand what the "unknowable void" in your fate really means.
It's not nothingness, {PlayerName}. All along, it's represented a future that can't be defined by anything but your own will.
As long as that void exists, you control your story. Your existence is the meaning itself.
So please, support Augusta with everything you have. I've seen how much she trusts you.
So trust her in return. No matter what happens, you are not to doubt her in the slightest.
That's the spirit.
Alright, you two. Stop standing around and get moving.
The sooner you settle this, the sooner I get to leave this boring place.
I... don't know.
Wait, Augusta!
I've been thinking... our final fate might already be written, but how we walk that path is up to us. We, as humans, are meant to carve our own way forward!
If we can't rewrite fate, then we chase it! Outpace it! Then, when we catch up, we'll smack it right in the face!
Don't forget what you once said: "A ruler assumes their place by accomplishing the impossible for their subjects."
After this, I shall likely sleep like I've never slept before.
Say, I heard the Montelli family imported some device that aids sleep. It's all the rage... Augusta, I trust you will acquire one for me? I wish to try it myself.
Scented candles are fine, though the scent of the forest is most pleasing. As for pillows and bedding...
Hmph. Apologies, but I need my rest if I'm to be at full power. Do bear with it. It's for your own good, after all.
And you, {PlayerName}? Were I to indulge in a bit of selfishness, you'd forgive me, wouldn't you?
Oh? Didn't think the great "Unwritten {PlayerName}" would be so stingy.
Well, lucky for you, I'm feeling generous today. Now, what brings you to me?
Really? You traveled all this way just to return what I gave you, is that it?
Was it truly such a burden for you?
If one day this bracelet becomes meaningless to anyone else, I hope you will remain the exception, Unwritten One.
But... perhaps in that distant future, forgetting might prove far easier than remembering. Never mind. In any case, you are not to return it.
It's something I've carried with me ever since I was bestowed the title of Priestess. Keep it for me, if only as a keepsake.
Well, until we meet again, I suppose.
Of course, only you would be the exception.
A death that shouldn't have happened, traded for an unforetold victory. A fair deal.
Now... I am but a fading sliver of moonlight.
I'm sorry, but...
Even I fear being...
Forgotten...
I could hear your voice from a distance, "Iuno, Iuno!" Wasn't that it?
Seems like you're in an awful hurry to find someone.
I thought you'd like this, us slipping back into our old, familiar rhythm... Now you know I'm still me. And yes, I really am back.
Exactly. See? I come right away and stand before you, in the flesh.
It's just like you saw at the end of the Chaos. Three years ago, at the end of the spring basin swallowed by the Dark Tide, I, as a Priestess, made my choice.
I was so done with just being dragged along by the moon's whims... So I defied fate, severed the path, and became the arrow that struck at destiny itself.
After that, I lost the ability to see clear visions of the future, but in return, I gained the power to anchor the Dark Tide's creations.
With that, the last prophecy I saw changed slightly. In a squad doomed to fall, one survived—Harelius.
If I can do it once, then I can do it a second time, then a third... Perhaps one day, I can overturn more destined futures.
Since fate didn't prepare a Hero of Heroes, we decided to eliminate the need for one by taking on the disaster at its source—the Sovereign and the Paradise.
This is the plan Augusta and I made, though I did hide the teeny tiny cost.
Anyway, all the Septimontians who were supposed to die are still alive. The end I once foresaw has changed. The meaning of this has already transcended my own existence.
So then, even if every trace of my "existence" was erased, who can say that the altered fate I left behind isn't proof of my existence?
More importantly, I had the privilege of standing by your side to witness what lies beyond the end. The void. An unwritten... void. Perhaps it signifies the unimaginable possibilities yet to come.
Just like you, bringing unknown possibilities to Septimont... and to me.
Why, sacrificing myself? Or just trying something I'm not sure about?
Come now, you know me... I follow my whims. And the more I trust someone, the harder it is to keep my impulses in check.
I'm not one to make promises I can't keep, but I'll promise you this... if there is a next time, you'll be the first to know.
And when I do, you'll get the full story. No holding back.
I've run into a few friends since being back. Not one of them recognized me.
Guess the memory of the Iuno they once knew is just... the price we had to pay.
But, at least... you still remember me. You pulled me from the Chaos and brought me back to existence. That's enough.
Oh, I'm certain Augusta and I, along with the rest of them, will be thick as thieves again, this time creating new futures. You really shouldn't underestimate my charm!
Oh yes, and this bracelet...
This is the second time I've given this to you. Never mind the first time, but I wouldn't refuse a second time, if I were you.
Being the second time means there's been a change... Now, it's a testament to our secret little interactions. I've Anchored the world, and you, you've Anchored me.
As for any matter pertaining to me... What you saw in the Chaos and what transpired, do not breathe a word of it to another soul. Understand?
Hmph, bashful? Hardly! If you blurt it out, it would cause quite a stir. All that explaining would be too much hassle.
Besides, to them, I am... quite the anomaly. If the truth came out, the first reaction would be shock.
You think so too? Okay. Good. I'm most relieved.
You think so too? Okay. Good. I'm most relieved.
Well, then again, this is a perfect opportunity to start anew.
In Septimont, it would be a crime to miss the height of the celebration.
After fighting side by side, we share honey and wine under a rain of petals, drinking to our hearts' content... Surely you've heard of this little tradition of ours, haven't you, Unwritten One?
Come on, let's go. Together... to be drenched in those rose petals that smell of glory and triumph.
